raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need to read file paths, get unique, make directories. Use bash: cat file | sort -u | xargs -I{} mkdir -p "$(dirname "{}")". But need to handle spaces. Use while read -r. Command: awk -F'\n' '...' but simpler: awk '!seen[$0]++{print $0}' file | while IFS= read -r p; do mkdir -p "$(dirname "$p")"; done. One line: awk '!seen[$0]++' /workspace/files.txt | while IFS= read -r p; do mkdir -p "$(dirname "$p")"; done.
